prevalence.value: ~0.02% annual reported incidence (substantial underreporting in some districts)
prevalence.source: National Center for Vector Borne Diseases Control (NCVBDC) / WHO
prevalence.trend: decreasing in recent years with intensified vector control; focal outbreaks persist

burden.mortalityRate: Low overall; severe disease concentrated in P. falciparum districts
burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) / WHO

riskFactors: poor housing and limited bed net use
delayed access to diagnosis and ACT
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rapid diagnostic test and/or microscopy with species identification; severe malaria criteria per national guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no routine screening; fever testing in endemic areas and during outbreak response
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high where RDT/ACT access is consistent; lower in remote tribal districts

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: RDT scale-up through NCVBDC and state programmes; microscopy confirmation in many districts
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ACT for P. falciparum; chloroquine for P. vivax where sensitive plus primaquine for radical cure; severe malaria managed with parenteral artesunate
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Heterogeneous burden across states, access constraints in tribal areas, and supply-chain gaps during peak season
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Large-scale vector control programmes (LLIN/IRS) and expanding community-level RDT/ACT availability
